headline: Horizon in Limbo: Costner's Epic Western Saga Stalls Despite Completed Footage
lede: Kevin Costner's ambitious four-part western epic 'Horizon' faces unprecedented distribution challenges, leaving industry insiders puzzled about its theatrical future.
body:
Hollywood's most anticipated independent western project continues to generate more questions than answers, as Kevin Costner's passion production remains shrouded in uncertainty.

Sources close to the production confirm that 'Horizon: Chapter 2' has been fully filmed, yet no official release strategy has emerged. The multi-part narrative, which Costner has personally financed and shepherded, represents a significant gamble in an increasingly risk-averse entertainment landscape.

Industry analysts suggest the project's complex financing and distribution model may be contributing to its prolonged limbo status. Costner's commitment to telling an expansive, multi-generational western saga appears undaunted, despite potential commercial headwinds.

The first chapter of 'Horizon' reportedly cost north of $80 million, with Costner personally investing substantial personal capital. His determination to control both creative and financial aspects of the project signals a bold departure from traditional studio filmmaking approaches.

Representatives for Costner have remained characteristically tight-lipped about potential release windows or distribution strategies. The actor-director's reputation for meticulous storytelling suggests he remains committed to releasing the project on his own precise terms.
key_facts: Second chapter fully filmed with no release date
Estimated first chapter budget exceeded $80 million
Independent production outside traditional studio model
